zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn於1227年出生在英國威爾斯丹比爾時，她的父親Gruffudd ap Iorwerth17歲，母親Margred III ferch Rhys17歲。她和Llewlyn ap Ynyr至少有1個兒子。她去世於1279年享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn于1227年出生在英国威尔斯丹比爾时，她的父亲Gruffudd ap Iorwerth17岁，母亲Margred III ferch Rhys17岁。她和Llewlyn ap Ynyr至少有1个儿子。她去世于1279年享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: Gruffydd
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Turkish: ornamental name or nickname from ay ‘Moon’, by extension also ‘beautiful face’.
Hungarian (Áy): unexplained.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 艾, see   Ai   2.

variants.name: Ai
meanings: Japanese: usually written 阿井 with characters used phonetically. The meaning could be ‘meeting’. In North America, it may also be a shortened form of some other name.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 艾, referring to ‘Chinese mugwort’, a type of plant used in traditional Chinese medical treatment: (i) perhaps from the second element of Ru Ai (汝艾), the name of an official who lived during the reign of Shao Kang, the 6th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ai Ling (艾陵), the name of a hill (located in Shandong province), the fief of Ai Kong (艾孔), an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the placename Ai (艾), the name of a fief (located in Jiangxi province) which was granted to an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) adopted as a surname by the Qu Jin (去斤), Qu Ai (去艾), and Ai Jin (艾斤) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (v) from Ai (艾), the name of an ancient state (possibly in present-day Jiangxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 愛, meaning ‘love’ in Chinese: (i) said to be traced back to Ai Xie Wu (愛邪勿), prime minister of the state of Huihu (known as Uyghur Empire or Uyghur Khaganate), who was endowed with the surname Ai (愛) by Emperor Wuzong of Tang (814–846  AD ). (ii) a Han Chinese surname for some members of the Aisin Gioro family from the Manchu ethnic group, known as the royal family of the Qing dynasty (1616 – 1911  AD ). (iii) a surname from minority ethnic groups in Taiwan.

nameMeanings.name: lowerth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Louth  .

variants.name: Louth
meanings: English: habitational name from Louth in Lincolnshire, socalled from its position on the river Lud (Old EnglishHlūde, meaning ‘the loud one’).
Irish: when not of English origin (see 1 above), probably a shortened and altered form of   McLeod  . Compare   McLouth  .

nameMeanings.name: leuaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English lef, leff, lief, life ‘beloved’ (Old English lēof ‘beloved; dear one, sweetheart’).

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English personal name Lef. This could represent either Old Norse Leifr (see Leif 1) or Old English Lēof which, though unrecorded, is a very probable short form of Old English names such as Lēofrīc, Lēofwine. Compare Leif 3.
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv: ornamental name from löv ‘leaf’. Compare Leif 2.

variants.name: Leif
meanings: Swedish: from the Old Norse personal name Leifr, a short form of the compound names with the second element leifr (from leif ‘heir’).
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv (see Leaf 3).
English: rare variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Levick
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Lefeke, Old English Lēofeca, a derivative of Lēofa (see Leaf ).
English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French l'eveske ‘the bishop’ (see Bishop ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Yiddish Leyvik, a pet form of the personal name Leyvi, itself a form of the Biblical name Levi (see Levy ).

variants.name: Leacock
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Levecok, a pet form of Leve, see Leaf .
variant of   Laycock  .

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Life
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Liff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yechiel, Chaim.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish: variant of   Lief  .

variants.name: Lief
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Varda, Meyer.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Livonia (now a part of Latvia), from German Liv.

variants.name: Leef
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn was born in 1227, in Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Gruffudd ap Iorwerth, was 17 and her mother, Margred III ferch Rhys, was 17. She had at least 1 son with Llewlyn ap Ynyr. She died in 1279, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn est née en 1227, à Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gruffudd ap Iorwerth, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Margred III ferch Rhys, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Llewlyn ap Ynyr. Elle est décédée en 1279, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn 1227 in Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gruffudd ap Iorwerth 17 und ihre Mutter Margred III ferch Rhys 17. Sie hatte mit Llewlyn ap Ynyr mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1279 im Alter von 52 Jahren.

ht.Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn è nata il nel 1227 nel Denbighshire, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Gruffudd ap Iorwerth, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Margred III ferch Rhys, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Llewlyn ap Ynyr. Morì nel 1279, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwynが1227年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアで生まれた時，父Gruffudd ap Iorwerthは17歳，母Margred III ferch Rhysは17歳であった。彼女はLlewlyn ap Ynyrとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1279年に52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn(이)가 1227년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gruffudd ap Iorwerth(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margred III ferch Rhys(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 Llewlyn ap Ynyr(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1279년 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn nasceu em 1227, em Denbighshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gruffudd ap Iorwerth, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Margred III ferch Rhys, tinha 17 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Llewlyn ap Ynyr. Ela faleceu em 1279, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn родилась в 1227 г. в местности денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gruffudd ap Iorwerth было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Margred III ferch Rhys, 17. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Llewlyn ap Ynyr. Она умерла в 1279 г. в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret ferch Gruffydd ab lowerth ab leuaf of Liwyn nació en 1227, en Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Gruffudd ap Iorwerth, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Margred III ferch Rhys, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Llewlyn ap Ynyr. Murió en 1279, a la edad de 52 años.
